cells.label: ergative
cells.function: ergative вАФ subject of a perfective transitive verb

conditioning: ANALYTIC: case is marked by a free POSTPOSITION after the (oblique-form) noun; а§Ха§Њ/а§Ха•З/а§Ха•А genitive agrees with the possessed noun's gender/number

fit_none.note: а§®а•З (ergative) has no English-adposition equivalent, so the alignment-based detector cannot label it; the others (а§Ха•Л/а§Єа•З/а§Ха§Њ/а§Ѓа•За§В/а§≤а§ња§ѓа•З) align 1:1 to of/to/in/from/for вАФ the expected gap

evidence_citations.claim: case is marked by closed-class postpositions
evidence_citations.stat: а§Ха•АвЖТof 0.90, а§Ха•ЛвЖТto 0.95, а§Ѓа•За§ВвЖТin 0.72, а§Єа•ЗвЖТfrom 0.66 (postposition's own alignment)
no suffixal case; the markers are separate high-frequency words following the noun

prose: hin marks case ANALYTICALLY with free POSTPOSITIONS after the (oblique) noun: ergative а§®а•З, accusative/dative а§Ха•Л, instrumental/ablative а§Єа•З, genitive а§Ха§Њ/а§Ха•З/а§Ха•А (agreeing in gender/number), locative а§Ѓа•За§В/а§™а§∞, benefactive а§Ха•З а§≤а§ња§ѓа•З. Because Hindi is SOV+postpositional and English is SVO+prepositional, role projection fails вАФ but each postposition aligns 1:1 to an English adposition (а§Ха•АвЖТof, а§Ха•ЛвЖТto, а§Ѓа•За§ВвЖТin, а§Єа•ЗвЖТfrom), so the case inventory is recovered from the postpositions' own alignment. Only ergative а§®а•З (no English equivalent) is missed. NOTE: this analysis depends on the Devanagari tokenizer fix (combining vowel-signs were previously stripped, shattering every word). Recommendation: PROMOTE а§Ха•Л/а§Єа•З/а§Ха§Њ/а§Ѓа•За§В/а§≤а§ња§ѓа•З; flag а§®а•З (ergative) as needing a vernacular-internal signal.
